name: Кино-афиша — Фрирен
subject: Frieren, a slender young-looking female elf with very long silver-white twin-tail hair, long pointed ears and calm green eyes
framing: Frieren occupies the central vertical axis as the single large dramatic hero figure, filling roughly the upper two-thirds of the sheet, framed from mid-thigh up at a low three-quarter angle so she looms heroically; her staff rises along the vertical. The film-title lockup sits centered in the lower third beneath her, tagline under it, greeked billing block at the base. A clean flat chroma field surrounds the whole composition on all four edges — no hair strand, staff tip or cape hem touches or bleeds off any edge.
render: Dramatic cinematic poster illustration with a single moody key light raking across the figure, deep atmospheric shadow, painterly anime-realist finish, volumetric haze and silver rim light — the look of a printed theatrical one-sheet.
moment: Theatrical one-sheet layout on a vertical poster field. TOP MARGIN: a single thin greeked tagline strip of pale-grey placeholder lettering (imitation text, not real words) runs across the very top. CENTER / UPPER TWO-THIRDS: Frieren stands as the large dramatic hero image on the central axis, framed from mid-thigh up at a low three-quarter angle so she reads monumental against a moody twilight sky and a distant blurred field of pale flowers. A single warm key light rakes from the upper left, throwing deep atmospheric shadow across her right side and catching a bright silver rim along her hair and staff. She is a slender, young-looking yet ancient elf: calm, faintly melancholic face with half-lidded pale-green eyes; VERY long silver-white hair bound in TWO side twin-tails drifting in the wind; long pointed elven ears; a white-cream mage's mantle with GOLD trim over a dark high-collared cape cinched by a belt; she holds a wooden staff topped with a pale crystal orb that glows softly as the poster's single brightest accent. LOWER THIRD: the big film-title lockup 'FRIEREN' is centered beneath her, the subtitle line set directly under it, with a small vertical Japanese caption フリーレン placed to one side. BOTTOM MARGIN: a dense greeked 'billing block' of tiny pale-grey imitation credit lettering (fake studio and cast credits, not readable words) spans the base, with one small greeked release-date line above it and two tiny placeholder laurel/logo marks. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two hands, correct anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure. A clean flat chroma field borders all four edges of the poster; no element touches or bleeds off any edge.
art_style: Формат — классический кино-постер (theatrical one-sheet): вертикальная афиша с крупной драматичной фигурой героя по центру, кинематографичным светом (один жёсткий key light, глубокие тени, дымка, rim-свет), большим title-логотипом фильма в нижней трети, tagline под ним и «billing block» кредитов у нижнего края. Визуальный язык — постерная живопись/аниме-реализм, приглушённая сумеречная гамма, ощущение трейлерного драматизма и премьеры.
typography: Main word 'FRIEREN' set once as the film-title lockup, centered in the lower third in a heavy high-contrast serif display face, cap-height about 0.8x the figure's shoulder width — the single largest text on the poster, appears exactly once. The secondary line 'BEYOND JOURNEY'S END' appears exactly once, sitting directly beneath the title in small letterspaced caps at roughly one-fifth the title height. The vertical フリーレン is small, set along one side near the title. IMPORTANT for OCR: only main + secondary (+ フリーレン) are real readable text and must stay short and exact; every other 'text' element — the top tagline strip, the bottom billing/credits block, the release-date line — is DECORATIVE greeked imitation only: thin pale-grey placeholder strokes suggesting text, never real words or letters. No other readable text beyond main and secondary.
palette: deep midnight blue, twilight violet, antique gold, warm cream, silver-white, soft ember amber
chroma_note: В дизайне нет заметной зелени (сумеречная сине-фиолетово-золотая гамма; зелёными остаются лишь небольшие глаза Фрирен внутри композиции, а не на краевом хромакей-поле), поэтому зелёный хромакей по краям безопасен и не конфликтует с палитрой постера. Синий/голубой ключ был бы опасен из-за midnight blue и twilight violet.

name: Обложка альбома / винила — Фрирен
subject: Frieren, a slender young-looking female elf with very long silver-white twin-tail hair, long pointed ears and calm green eyes
framing: Iconic square record-sleeve layout, strictly frontal and symmetrical like a classic art-pop LP cover. Frieren is the single central cover image: a large, poster-flat bust-and-shoulders portrait placed dead-center on a wide flat field of colour, occupying roughly the middle 55 percent of the square. She faces forward with a calm, faintly melancholic half-lidded gaze, her very long silver-white twin-tail hair cascading straight down past her shoulders and framing the composition like two vertical silver ribbons. The bold artist/album wordmark sits across the very top of the sleeve as a horizontal masthead band; a small label logo and catalogue number sit as tiny corner marks. A single thin vertical Japanese caption runs down one side. The record sleeve is a square panel with its OWN flat sage-green colour field; that whole sleeve panel sits centered on a SEPARATE plain blue chroma margin framing all four sides, so nothing — hair tips, shoulders, staff — touches or bleeds off any edge, with a generous quiet blue chroma margin all around for clean keying (the sage-green sleeve field itself is kept, never keyed).
render: Bold graphic album-cover art: flat poster-like screenprint look, limited-palette risograph/art-pop LP aesthetic, crisp hard-edged shapes, minimal shading, high-contrast silhouette against a single flat colour field, memorable and iconic like a collectible vinyl sleeve.
moment: A square vinyl record-sleeve, read instantly as an art-pop LP cover. LAYOUT: across the very top runs a horizontal MASTHEAD band carrying the big artist/album wordmark "FRIEREN" (main) in a bold flat sans; directly under it, centered and small, one tagline line "BEYOND JOURNEY'S END" (secondary). Top-left corner: a tiny circular record-label mark rendered as an abstract flat glyph. Top-right corner: a small catalogue tag shown as a short greeked code (thin grey placeholder characters, not real words). Down the right edge runs a single slim vertical Japanese caption "フリーレン". Bottom edge kept as a quiet flat strip with a faint greeked barcode block and a tiny greeked side-line credit — decorative stand-in strokes only, no real letters. CENTER: the album's hero image is Frieren as a large flat frontal bust portrait. She is a slender young-looking elf with a calm, faintly melancholic expression and pale light-green half-lidded eyes; very long silver-white hair worn in TWO side twin-tails falling straight down; long pointed elf ears; she wears the mage outfit — a white-cream mantle with GOLD trim over a dark high-collared cape and a belt. One hand rests near the top of a plain wooden staff topped with a pale glowing crystal orb, the orb reading as a soft graphic circle of light beside her. Flat poster colour blocking, a few flower silhouettes drifting as small accent shapes at her shoulders to echo the fields-of-flowers theme. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two hands, correct anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure. Keep the sleeve's own background field flat sage-green, and set that whole square sleeve on a SEPARATE plain blue chroma margin on all four sides so nothing touches or bleeds off any edge, with a generous quiet blue chroma margin all around for clean keying — the sage-green sleeve field is part of the art and must never be keyed out.
art_style: Формат — квадратная обложка музыкального альбома / винилового конверта. Визуальный язык: арт-поп LP, плоская трафаретная/рисограф-печать, ограниченная палитра, жёсткие контуры, крупный графичный центральный образ и типографская плашка-masthead сверху. Иконичность коллекционного винила, читается с первого взгляда как «обложка пластинки», а не иллюстрация в рамке.
typography: Main wordmark "FRIEREN" sits in the top masthead band, bold flat geometric sans, all-caps, cap-height about 0.9x the figure's shoulder width, spanning the width of the sleeve, appears exactly once. Secondary line "BEYOND JOURNEY'S END" is centered directly beneath it in a thin light sans, cap-height about 0.22x that of the main wordmark, letter-spaced wide, appears exactly once. Vertical "フリーレン" runs down the right edge at small size, appears exactly once. IMPORTANT for OCR: only main + secondary (+ フリーレン) are real readable text and must stay short and exact. Everything else that looks like text — the corner catalogue code, the label glyph, the bottom barcode block and side credit line — is DECORATIVE IMITATION of text only: thin grey greeked placeholder strokes, not real words, so OCR is not disrupted. No other readable text beyond main and secondary.
palette: warm cream, soft ivory-white hair, muted sage green field, gold-ochre trim, deep charcoal-teal cape, dusty rose flower accents
chroma_note: Фон-поле обложки — приглушённый шалфейно-зелёный, плюс золотисто-зелёные акценты палитры, поэтому ключ синий, чтобы зелёный фон и оторочка не выедались хромакеем. Синий хромакей — только внешний margin вокруг квадратного конверта; само шалфейно-зелёное поле обложки сохраняется и не кеится.

name: Журнальная обложка — Фрирен
subject: Frieren, a slender young-looking female elf with very long silver-white twin-tail hair, long pointed ears and calm green eyes
framing: Glossy fashion-magazine cover layout on a single vertical portrait page whose paper is a flat muted sage-green panel. Frieren is the cover model, framed from mid-chest up and shifted slightly left of center, her calm melancholic face turned three-quarters toward the viewer with half-lidded pale green eyes. The huge masthead runs straight across the very top of the page; the crown and front sweep of her silver-white hair, plus the tip of one long pointed ear, rise UP and overlap IN FRONT of the masthead letters so she reads as physically standing before the title, not behind flat text. Her two long silver-white twin-tails fall down both sides of the page past her shoulders. Short vertical cover-lines sit stacked down the left and right margins of the page beside her; a barcode with an issue date sits in the lower corner. The whole sage-green magazine page floats centered on a clean flat solid BLUE chroma field with a generous even empty margin on every side — the page, and no hair strand, ear or graphic element, touches any edge of the frame.
render: clean glossy fashion-magazine cover styling: smooth editorial cover-photo rendering of the figure with soft even studio light, the magazine page rendered as a flat solid muted sage-green panel with a subtle glossy print sheen, crisp printed masthead and cover-line typography, the entire page set on a flat solid blue chroma backdrop for clean extraction
moment: A glossy newsstand fashion-magazine cover, a single vertical portrait page whose paper is a flat muted sage-green panel. LAYOUT: a single bold sans-serif MASTHEAD spans the full width across the very top of the page in large heavy caps — the biggest text on the cover. Directly beneath the left end of the masthead sits one small readable subtitle line (secondary). Down the LEFT margin and the RIGHT margin of the page run short stacked cover-lines rendered as thin light-grey greeked filler strokes (decorative imitation text, not real words), each with a tiny dot or number bullet, mimicking teaser headlines. The lower-left or lower-right corner carries a printed barcode block with a short issue date beside it, both drawn as fine grey line-work. FIGURE: Frieren is the cover model, centered slightly left, shown from mid-chest up. She is a slender young-looking elf with a calm, faintly melancholic expression and half-lidded pale light-green eyes. Her hair is very long, silver-white, parted and tied into TWO side twin-tails that spill down past her shoulders on both sides; the top sweep of her hair and the tip of one long pointed elf ear cross UP and overlap in front of the masthead lettering, so she stands before the title. She wears a mage's white-and-cream mantle with a golden trim border and a dark high-collared cape over it with a belt. Soft even studio lighting, glossy editorial finish. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two hands, correct anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure. The whole sage-green magazine page floats centered on a clean flat solid BLUE chroma field with an even empty margin — the page, and no hair, ear, or graphic element, touches any edge of the frame.
art_style: Глянцевая журнальная обложка (fashion/newsstand cover): язык модного глянца — крупный сплошной masthead-логотип во всю ширину сверху, cover-модель за ним, вертикальные плашки-тизеры по бокам, штрихкод с датой в углу, гладкая печать со студийным светом и лёгким лаковым блеском. Сама страница журнала — приглушённо-шалфейно-зелёная глянцевая бумага, и она целиком сохраняется как принт. Читается как обложка журнала с первого взгляда, а не иллюстрация в рамке.
typography: MAIN word "MAGE" is the masthead: heavy bold sans-serif all-caps, spanning almost the full page width across the very top, cap-height about 0.9x the figure's shoulder width, appears exactly once; the top sweep of Frieren's silver-white hair and one pointed ear-tip overlap in front of its letters. SECONDARY line "THE ETERNAL ISSUE" is a single small caps subtitle tucked directly under the left end of the masthead, cap-height about 0.12x the masthead height, appears exactly once. Vertical katakana フリーレン runs small down one side margin as a slim accent. All other magazine text — the side cover-lines, teaser bullets, the date beside the barcode — is rendered as DECORATIVE greeked imitation only: thin light-grey placeholder strokes suggesting words, never actual legible letters, so OCR reads nothing there. No other readable text beyond main and secondary.
palette: cream white, warm ivory, gold trim, deep charcoal cape, muted sage-green page background, soft silver-white hair
chroma_note: Ключ — BLUE. Вся журнальная страница (кремово-белая мантия с золотой оторочкой, тёмная накидка с высоким воротом, серебристо-белые волосы, бледно-зелёные глаза и приглушённо-шалфейный фон самой страницы) — это и есть принт, она должна сохраниться целиком, поэтому зелёный фон-страницу ключить нельзя. Вокруг страницы — ровное плоское СИНЕЕ поле хромакея с пустым отступом по всем краям. Синего нет нигде — ни на Фрирен, ни на обложке — поэтому синий ключ чисто вырезает всю обложку вместе с её шалфейно-зелёной страницей, а бледно-зелёные глаза и зелёный фон остаются нетронутыми.

name: Спорт-джерси / varsity — Фрирен
subject: Frieren, a slender young-looking female elf with very long silver-white twin-tail hair, long pointed ears and calm green eyes
framing: Athletic varsity-jersey graphic layout, centered on a clean chroma field. The composition is dominated by a giant collegiate number stacked mid-chest; the character is NOT a full drawn figure but appears only as a small circular crest-patch emblem (Frieren's head-and-staff silhouette) sewn near the top, plus a matching tiny shoulder patch. Frieren-as-emblem is minimal and iconic — the jersey typography and number own the frame. Everything sits well inside a wide empty chroma margin; no element, patch, letter or number touches any edge.
render: Flat collegiate athletic screen-print look: bold varsity block letters and a huge felt-and-twill applique number with visible chain-stitch outline, tackle-twill patch texture on the crest, matte flocked fill, subtle stitched drop-shadow — no photo realism, pure heavyweight-jersey print graphics.
moment: A classic American varsity / college athletic jersey graphic laid out vertically and symmetrically on an empty chroma field. TOP: the team name FRIEREN set in a bold upward-arching collegiate block wordmark, felt-tackle-twill style with a contrast chain-stitch outline. Directly BELOW the arch, centered, a small round embroidered crest-patch: a stylized emblem of Frieren rendered as a compact heraldic badge — her calm young elven face with polar-white twin-tail hair sweeping to both sides, long pointed ears, half-lidded green eyes, and her wooden staff with a pale crystal orb crossing behind, all reduced to a two-color twill applique inside a stitched ring. CENTER, filling the mid-chest, a GIANT athletic number "1000" (a thousand years) in stacked varsity block digits with double felt outline and chain-stitch trim — the dominant graphic mass of the whole print. A slim vertical katakana tab フリーレン runs down one side of the number like a sleeve stripe. Two thin twin racing stripes flank the number left and right. A tiny second shoulder-style patch of the staff-and-crystal sits at upper corner, balanced by faint greeked lettering imitating a college-athletic tagline arc at the very bottom. Small scattered five-pointed field-flower motifs act as decorative athletic stars. Colors stay in cream, gold-twill, deep charcoal-navy and soft sage. ANATOMY: Frieren appears ONLY as a small circular crest emblem (her calm elven face plus the crossing staff-and-crystal), never as a full body — no torso, arms or legs are drawn; the crest is a single flat two-color twill applique, and any implied hand carries five fingers; no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure, no duplicated crest. A wide clean chroma-key field surrounds everything; nothing touches the edges.
art_style: Формат — спортивная студенческая джерси (varsity / college athletic). Визуальный язык: гигантский центральный номер, имя команды дугой сверху, эмблема-нашивка (crest patch) из твила и фетра, chain-stitch обводки, tackle-twill аппликация, атлетические блочные шрифты, гоночные полосы. Плоская трафаретная печать спортивной формы — не иллюстрация персонажа, а джерси-графика, где типографика и номер доминируют, а Фрирен присутствует лишь маленькой геральдической нашивкой.
typography: Two athletic type roles only. MAIN — the giant number "1000" in stacked collegiate varsity block digits, cap-height about 0.55x the figure width, dead-centered as the print's dominant mass, appears exactly once. SECONDARY — the arched team wordmark "FRIEREN" in bold collegiate block caps, cap-height about 0.12x the figure width, curved above the crest, appears exactly once. The vertical katakana フリーレン runs small as a side sleeve-stripe tab. All three (1000, FRIEREN, フリーレン) are the only genuinely readable text and are kept short and crisp for clean OCR. Any other apparent lettering — the bottom tagline arc and any small athletic sub-labels — is DECORATIVE GREEKED IMITATION: thin faint stitched placeholder strokes suggesting words, NOT real characters. No other readable text beyond main and secondary.
palette: cream ivory, gold twill, deep charcoal-navy, soft sage green, muted stitch-white
chroma_note: В дизайне мягкий шалфейно-зелёный — рабочий цвет палитры (гоночные полосы/акценты) плюс зелёные глаза Фрирен, поэтому зелёный хромакей выедал бы эти детали принта при кеинге. Фон делаем синим.

name: Концертный флаер / тур-постер — Фрирен
subject: Frieren, a slender young-looking female elf with very long silver-white hair bound in TWO side twin-tails, long pointed ears and calm half-lidded pale green eyes
framing: A rock concert gig-poster. Frieren is the poster hero: one large, dramatic three-quarter figure occupying the central lower two-thirds, rendered in bold posterized silk-screen graphic — flat punchy colour fills, heavy ink outlines, halftone grit. She is framed from mid-thigh up, staff angled diagonally across the composition like a guitar-neck line of energy, hair sweeping into decorative rock-poster flourishes and radiating line-burst rays behind her head. The giant band-name lockup FRIEREN arches across the very top as the masthead, secondary tour line sits just beneath it, and a decoratively-greeked list of tour dates and city names runs in tight columns along the bottom margin. All edges are clean chroma-key field — figure, type and rays float free and never touch the border.
render: Energetic silk-screen gig-poster graphics: limited punchy spot colours, flat posterized fills, thick black keyline ink, coarse halftone dots and light misregister/riso texture, screenprint grain and radiating ray-bursts. The large sunburst ray fields are printed in WARM spot inks (hot magenta, gold ochre, bone cream); deep teal is used only as a small local accent on the figure and outfit, never as a large background field or near the edges, so the green chroma key stays clean.
moment: LAYOUT — a classic rock gig-poster reading top-to-bottom. TOP BAND: the masthead word FRIEREN arches in a huge condensed slab display face spanning the full width, treated as a screenprinted band-name lockup with a slight arch and heavy drop, the largest element on the sheet. Directly under it a single bold banner line WORLD TOUR with a small greeked tour subtitle. CENTRE: Frieren as the poster hero in bold posterized silk-screen style — flat colour blocking, thick ink outlines, halftone shading. She stands three-quarter turned, calm faintly melancholic face with half-lidded pale green eyes, VERY long silver-white hair bound in TWO side twin-tails that flare out into rock-poster flourishes, long pointed elf ears clearly visible. She wears the mage outfit: white/cream mantle with GOLD trim over a dark high-collared cape and a belt; her wooden staff with a pale crystal orb is angled diagonally across the frame, the orb glowing with a burst of screenprinted rays behind her printed in warm magenta and gold spot inks. RADIATING sunburst rays and starbursts in warm magenta, gold and bone-cream spot inks explode outward behind the figure; any deep teal stays a small local accent on her clothing, not a large field. BOTTOM MARGIN: a dense tour-date list — city + venue rows in tight small columns — rendered as decorative greeked text (thin grey placeholder line-strokes, not real words). Small greeked promoter/credit block and a faux barcode sit in a bottom corner. A single small vertical Japanese katakana strip フリーレン runs down one side. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two hands, correct anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure. All artwork sits on a clean chroma-key field with nothing touching the edges.
art_style: Это концертный гиг-постер / тур-плакат рок-группы. Визуальный язык шелкографии: ограниченная бьющая палитра плоских спот-цветов, толстые чёрные контуры-кейлайны, крупный halftone-растр и лёгкий riso-мисрегистр, лучи-солнце из-за фигуры в тёплых цветах, арочный «band-name» логотип сверху и колонки дат тура снизу — энергия концертного плаката, читается с первого взгляда.
typography: Main lockup FRIEREN in a huge condensed slab/display gig-poster face, arched across the top masthead, cap-height about 0.9x the figure width, appears exactly once as the band name. Secondary WORLD TOUR set as one bold banner line directly beneath, cap-height about 0.28x the figure width, appears exactly once. The single vertical katakana strip フリーレン runs small down one side. IMPORTANT for OCR: only main + secondary (+ フリーレン) are real readable text and must stay short and exact. Everything else — the entire tour-date/city list, the venue rows, the promoter/credit block, the barcode digits, any small tagline or subtitle — is DECORATIVE GREEKED text: thin grey placeholder line-strokes that only imitate typography, NOT real words. Explicitly render that filler as greeked stand-in text so OCR reads nothing there. No other readable text beyond main and secondary.
palette: ink black, bone cream, silver-white, gold ochre, deep teal, hot magenta
chroma_note: Зелени в палитре мало (только тёмный teal-акцент на фигуре, не сплошной и не у краёв); крупные лучи-солнце и вспышки за фигурой сделаны в тёплых спот-цветах (magenta/gold/cream), фон — чистый хромакей, поэтому зелёный ключ безопасен; deep teal локальный, малый, не сплошной.

name: Газетная передовица — Фрирен
subject: Frieren, a slender young-looking female elf with very long silver-white twin-tail hair, long pointed ears and calm green eyes
framing: Frieren appears only as a boxed halftone photograph set into the front page's upper-left columns — a portrait-vignette press insert, not a full-bleed figure — so the newspaper layout, not the character, dominates the composition. The whole broadsheet page sits centered on a clean solid chroma field with even margins; nothing touches the edges.
render: Vintage newsprint front-page print: coarse halftone dot photo, flat letterpress ink-on-paper look, grey greeked column rules, subtle paper grain and a slight registration wobble on the ink.
moment: Full vintage broadsheet front page, portrait orientation, printed on aged cream newsprint. TOP: the masthead "THE CHRONICLE" runs in large blackletter-serif capitals across the entire top width, bracketed by two thin horizontal ink rules. Directly beneath sits a dateline bar with a tiny greeked edition line, a faux date and a price mark rendered as thin grey placeholder strokes, not real words. Below the masthead a single bold banner headline stretches across the top columns. The lower two-thirds is divided into four to five narrow vertical columns of greeked body text — even grey lines of nonsense typographic imitation with the occasional small greeked sub-head and a drop cap. Set into the columns in the upper-left quadrant is a boxed halftone photograph of Frieren: a coarse black-and-white dot-screen portrait framed by a thin keyline with a slim greeked caption strip underneath. In the photo Frieren is a slender young-looking elf with a calm, faintly melancholic face and half-lidded pale-green eyes, very long silver-white hair gathered into two side twin-tails, long pointed elven ears, wearing a white-and-cream mage robe with gold trim and a dark high-collared cape with a belt; a wooden staff topped by a pale crystal orb rests at her shoulder. A small vertical kanji stamp reading フリーレン sits low in a column like an edition mark. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two hands, correct anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure. The entire page floats centered on a clean solid chroma field with even margin so no element touches the edges.
art_style: Формат — первая полоса винтажной газеты (broadsheet). Визуальный язык прессовой полиграфии: masthead-логотип газеты, баннерный заголовок, узкие колонки набора, halftone-фотоврезка с подписью, тонкие линейки-рулы, датлайн с ценой. Читается как газетная передовица с первого взгляда, а не как иллюстрация в декоративной рамке.

text_content.secondary: THE MAGE WHO OUTLIVED THE HERO

typography: Main "THE CHRONICLE" is the masthead in heavy blackletter-serif capitals spanning the full page width, cap-height about 0.13x page width, appears exactly once at the very top. Secondary "THE MAGE WHO OUTLIVED THE HERO" is the single bold banner headline just below the masthead, cap-height about 0.05x page width, appears exactly once. Only main, secondary and the small vertical フリーレン stamp are real readable type; every other element — dateline, price, photo caption, sub-heads and all body columns — is purely decorative greeked text: thin grey placeholder strokes imitating words, not actual letters, so OCR reads nothing there. No other readable text beyond main and secondary.
palette: aged newsprint cream, ink black, warm halftone grey, faded sepia brown, muted sage-green accent, tarnished gold
chroma_note: Газета почти монохромная (кремово-серая), заметной зелени в самой полосе нет — зелёный хромакей-фон безопасен и не конфликтует с приглушённым сажевым акцентом глаз и оторочки.

name: Винтажная реклама — Фрирен
subject: Frieren, a slender young-looking female elf with very long silver-white twin-tail hair, long pointed ears and calm green eyes
framing: Frieren is the advertisement's "brand figure", placed on the central vertical axis and shown from the waist up at medium scale — small enough that the arched product-name masthead dominates above her and the greeked fine print sits below her, exactly as a Belle Epoque product model would be staged inside a chromolithograph poster. She is enclosed by ornamental Art-Nouveau borders yet never touches them: a clean chroma-key margin surrounds the entire sheet on every side, and nothing bleeds to the edge.
render: Vintage chromolithograph advertisement poster: warm aged and lightly foxed paper tone, slightly off-register retro ad inks, flat lithographic shading, Art-Nouveau ornamental framing and retro apothecary ad typography.
moment: A turn-of-the-century apothecary advertisement laid out as an old chromolithograph "elixir" poster. TOP THIRD: an ornate arched masthead ribbon carries the large product name across the sheet, flanked by Art-Nouveau leaf flourishes, with a small circular award-medallion seal tucked into one upper corner (imitation "prize medal" of old ads). CENTER: Frieren rendered in vintage ad-poster styling, a slender young-looking elf seen from the waist up and turned three-quarter, calmly presenting a small ornate amber vial of the elixir in one raised hand while her other hand loosely holds a wooden staff topped with a pale crystal orb. Her expression is serene and faintly melancholic with half-lidded light-green eyes; her very long silver-white hair falls in TWO distinct side twin-tails; she has long pointed elf ears; she wears a cream-white mage robe with GOLD trim beneath a dark high-collared cape gathered at a belt. BELOW THE FIGURE: a small tagline banner, then two narrow columns of greeked fine print imitating an ingredient list and directions, a decorative "since" date line in faux roman numerals, and scrolled corner ornaments. A slim vertical maker's-mark stamp runs down one side. The whole sheet reads at a glance as a warm-toned antique product advertisement. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two hands, correct anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure. A clean chroma-key field surrounds all edges — nothing touches the border.
art_style: Старинный рекламный плакат-этикетка аптечного «эликсира» в духе литографий рубежа XIX–XX веков (ар-нуво / Бель Эпок): орнаментальные арочные плашки и рамки, наградной медальон, узкие колонки мелкого шрифта, тёплая выцветшая типографика — формат читается сразу как ретро-реклама продукта, а не как иллюстрация в декоративной рамке.
typography: MAIN — "ELIXIR OF AGES" — set once in an ornate condensed lithograph serif, arched along the masthead banner across the upper third, cap-height about 0.8x the figure's shoulder width, appears exactly once. SECONDARY — "A SIP OF ETERNITY" — one italic display-serif line on a small banner just below the figure, roughly one-third the main cap-height, appears exactly once. The vertical フリーレン runs as a slim maker's-mark stamp down one side. All remaining "text" (ingredient list, directions, address, since-date, medallion motto) is DECORATIVE IMITATION ONLY — thin faded grey greeked line-fills, not real words — so it never competes for legibility. No other readable text beyond main and secondary.
palette: warm sepia brown, aged parchment cream, faded antique gold/ochre, muted terracotta red, soft ink-black, dusty amber
chroma_note: Зелёного в кадре почти нет — только светло-зелёные глаза как мелкий акцент; фон и вся палитра тёплые (сепия, крем, золото/охра, терракота, амбра). Золотая оторочка и охра держатся в тёпло-жёлтой зоне и не уходят в жёлто-зелёный, поэтому безопасно ключить по зелёному хромакею, не задев наряд.

name: Wanted / розыскной плакат — Фрирен
subject: Frieren, a slender young-looking female elf with very long silver-white twin-tail hair, long pointed ears and calm green eyes
framing: Old-west WANTED poster layout: a giant "WANTED" header spans the top; centered below sits a framed oval medallion portrait-vignette of Frieren from the chest up, rendered as an engraved halftone bust; a bold "REWARD" line with a coin sum sits under the medallion; thin greeked filler lines flank and fill the margins as fake broadsheet copy. Frieren occupies only the central oval — a small dignified bust, not a full dramatic figure — so the poster format (header + medallion + reward) reads first. Aged torn broadsheet paper fills the field. Everything floats on a clean flat chroma-key background with generous empty margin on all four sides; the paper edges, torn corners and all type stay well inside and never touch the border.
render: Aged wanted-poster print: vintage letterpress broadsheet look, an engraved copperplate / crosshatch halftone portrait inside the oval medallion, heavy distressed woodtype headers, sepia-and-ink flat printing with foxing, water stains and a torn, curling paper texture.
moment: A single old-west WANTED broadsheet, portrait orientation, printed on aged cream paper with foxing spots, water rings, worn creases and roughly torn, slightly curling edges. LAYOUT top-to-bottom: (1) MASTHEAD — the word "WANTED" in massive distressed slab-serif woodtype straddling the full top width, ink cracked and uneven from old letterpress. (2) A single thin greeked sub-band directly under it (fake "dead or alive" style filler — tiny grey placeholder strokes, not real words). (3) CENTRAL MEDALLION — a decorative oval frame with rope-and-scroll border holding an engraved crosshatch bust portrait of Frieren from the chest up, face three-quarter, printed in sepia ink halftone. Frieren canon: a slender youthful-looking elf with a calm, faintly melancholic expression and half-lidded pale green eyes; very long silver-white hair worn in two side twin-tails framing the oval; long pointed elven ears; mage attire — a white/cream mantle with gold trim and a dark high-collared cape with a belt; no staff needed in this tight bust crop. (4) REWARD BLOCK — the word "REWARD" in bold condensed serif, a large coin-sum beneath it, and a small engraved coin/star ornament. (5) FOOTER & MARGINS — several thin horizontal greeked lines imitating printed broadsheet body copy, plus a faint printer's-mark ornament, all rendered as decorative grey placeholder text, never real letters. A small vertical katakana caption フリーレン sits discreetly near the reward line. ANATOMY: a single figure only — one Frieren, one face; correct anatomy for a chest-up bust, hands fall outside this tight crop; no extra, duplicated or malformed limbs and no second figure. The whole torn poster sits on a clean flat chroma-key field with wide empty margins on every side; nothing — paper, type or medallion — touches the edges.
art_style: Формат — розыскной плакат («WANTED») в стиле старого вестерна: полиграфия XIX века. Визуальный язык: массивный woodtype-заголовок сверху, овальный медальон-врезка с гравюрным (crosshatch/halftone) портретом по центру, строка «REWARD» с суммой под ним, декоративные «газетные» строки в полях, состаренная рваная бумага с пятнами и потёртостями, сепия + чёрная типографская краска. Раскладка «шапка → медальон → награда» узнаётся с первого взгляда и не похожа на фигуру в декоративной рамке.
typography: "WANTED" is the single largest element — distressed slab-serif woodtype across the top, cap-height about 0.9x the poster's figure width, appears exactly once. "REWARD 50000" is the secondary line in bold condensed serif under the medallion, cap-height about 0.28x figure width, appears exactly once. The vertical katakana フリーレン is small near the reward line. IMPORTANT for OCR: only main (WANTED) and secondary (REWARD 50000) plus フリーレン are real readable text — keep them short and crisp. Every other "poster text" — the dead-or-alive sub-band, the broadsheet body lines, the footer printer's marks and any margin copy — must be rendered as DECORATIVE IMITATION of text only: thin faint grey greeked placeholder strokes with no real letters or words, so OCR is never triggered. No other readable text beyond main and secondary.
palette: aged cream paper, sepia brown ink, deep charcoal black woodtype, faded gold trim accent, muted foxing tan, pale silver-white hair highlight
chroma_note: Палитра тёплая (кремовая бумага, сепия, чёрная краска, приглушённое золото) — зелени в фоне/дизайне нет, поэтому хромакей зелёный по умолчанию; единственная зелень — светло-зелёные глаза Фрирен, она мелкая и не мешает.

name: Стикер-пак — Фрирен
subject: Frieren, a slender young-looking female elf with very long silver-white twin-tail hair, long pointed ears and calm green eyes
framing: A die-cut sticker sheet, not a single portrait: six or seven separate small Frieren motifs scattered loosely across a flat chroma field, each motif isolated on its own thick white die-cut border with a soft drop shadow, playfully tilted at different angles like peel-off stickers. Motifs vary in scale (chibi bust largest, name-badge and sparkle smallest) and every sticker floats fully inside the frame with generous empty chroma between them and a wide clean margin, so nothing touches or bleeds off the edges.
render: Flat kawaii sticker art with thick uniform white die-cut outlines around each motif, bold clean cel-shading, minimal gradients, crisp vector-style line and a subtle glossy sticker sheen — cute merch-sheet illustration, not a painterly poster.
moment: Layout reads instantly as a peel-and-stick die-cut sticker sheet: 6–7 individual stickers scattered and gently rotated across an empty chroma field, each wrapped in a fat white die-cut border with a faint grey drop shadow. TOP-LEFT, the largest sticker: a chibi Frieren bust — big round head, calm half-lidded pale green eyes, tiny serene almost-bored smile, very long silver-white hair in two side twin-tails, long pointed elf ears, wearing her cream-white mage mantle with golden trim and a dark high-collared charcoal-navy cape. CENTER-RIGHT: a slim standalone sticker of her wooden staff topped by a pale lilac crystal orb, catching a small star-shine. LOWER-LEFT: a round crystal ball sticker glowing soft lilac. CENTER: a ribbon-banner name-badge sticker reading the main word once in bold. UPPER-RIGHT: a tiny full chibi figure of Frieren standing with the staff, mantle and twin-tails visible. SCATTERED between them: a small blue field flower, a four-point sparkle star, and a slim vertical tag sticker carrying the katakana. Whole sheet feels light, cute and collectible, colours flat and cheerful. ANATOMY: within each individual sticker Frieren has exactly two arms and two hands, correct anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs and no ghosted double of the figure inside a single sticker; the several separate Frieren stickers across the sheet (chibi bust and full chibi figure) are intended and correct. All stickers float fully inside a clean chroma field with a wide empty margin so nothing touches the edges.
art_style: Формат — лист die-cut наклеек (kawaii sticker sheet). Визуальный язык мерч-полиграфии: плоская векторная заливка, толстая белая высечная обводка вокруг каждого мотива, лёгкая тень и глянцевый блик «наклейки». Несколько отдельных мелких мотивов, разбросанных по листу, а не одна цельная иллюстрация — читается как набор стикеров с первого взгляда.
typography: Main word "FRIEREN" appears exactly once, set as bold rounded kawaii caps on the central ribbon name-badge sticker, cap-height about 0.9x the width of the chibi-bust sticker — the single biggest lettering on the sheet. Secondary line "Beyond Journey's End" appears exactly once, sitting directly beneath it in a much smaller light script, roughly one-quarter the cap-height of main. Vertical katakana フリーレン runs once down the slim tag sticker in small clean strokes. Any other tiny marks on the stickers (mini cover-line squiggles, filler characters, sparkle glyphs) are DECORATIVE greeked imitation only — faint thin grey placeholder strokes, NOT real words — so OCR reads nothing there. No other readable text beyond main and secondary.
palette: cream white, warm gold, deep charcoal-navy, pale lilac crystal, soft blush pink, silver-white hair
chroma_note: Зелени в мотивах почти нет — мантия кремово-белая с золотом, накидка тёмно-сине-угольная, кристалл бледно-лиловый, цветок синий; зелёный хромакей-фон безопасен, ничего из палитры с ним не сольётся. Единственный зелёный — крошечные бледно-зелёные глаза Фрирен — полностью заключён внутри белых die-cut обводок и не касается фона, поэтому не выбивается кеингом.
